title: Adding user-defined keyboard shortcuts in the IDE
breadcrumbs: Adding user-defined keyboard shortcuts in the IDE
text: Adding user-defined keyboard shortcuts in the IDE You can use the following default shortcut keys in the IDE to navigate inside the editor or trigger various supported actions with a keyboard, such as compile a current object or compile and test an object. You can also modify and define your own shortcut key combinations for the supported actions in the [userkeys] section of the usys.ini file. Explain this code [userkeys] nav.ubar=Alt+D nav.message.frame=Ctrl+M editor.refresh=F5 editor.close=Ctrl+F4 editor.close.all=Ctrl+Shift+F4 editor.next=Ctrl+Tab editor.previous=Ctrl+Shift+Tab build.compile=Ctrl+F7 build.test=Ctrl+F5 build.compile.test=Ctrl+Shift+F7 code.comment=Ctrl+K code.uncomment=Ctrl+Shift+K code.uppercase=Ctrl+U code.lowercase=Ctrl+Shift+U code.formatselection=Alt+F code.formatdocument=Alt+Shift+F code.search=Ctrl+F code.searchandreplace=Ctrl+H code.gotoline=Ctrl+G Copy Search: Column Action Default shortcut key combination Usage Filter results... 19 rows found Table 1. Default keyboard combination's description Action Default shortcut key combination Usage Filter 19 rows found Filter 19 rows found Filter 19 rows found Action Default shortcut key combination Usage nav.ubar Alt+D To navigate to the main U-Bar. The current U-Bar content is also selected, allowing you to start typing directly. nav.message.frame Ctrl+M To move focus to the next message frame editor.refresh F5 To refresh the current editor window editor.close Ctrl+F4 To close the current editor tab editor.close.all Ctrl+Shift+F4 To close all editor tabs editor.next Ctrl+Tab To open the next editor tab editor.previous Ctrl+Shift+Tab To open the previous editor tab build.compile Ctrl+F7 To compile the current object build.test Ctrl+F5 To test the current component build.compile.test Ctrl+Shift+F7 To compile and test the current object code.comment Ctrl+K To comment out a code code.uncomment Ctrl+Shift+K To uncomment out a code code.uppercase Ctrl+U To uppercase a selection of code code.lowercase Ctrl+Shift+U To lowercase a selection of code code.formatselection Alt+F To format and indent a selection of code code.formatdocument Alt+Shift+F To format an entire document from the first to the last line code.search Ctrl+F To search a string in code code.searchandreplace Ctrl+H To search and replace a string in code code.gotoline Ctrl+G To go to a line number in code Table 2. Related learning videos Title link IDE Keyboard Shortcuts Related Links [userkeys] You can add user-defined keyboard shortcut keys for various Uniface IDE actions or your applications in the [userkeys] section of the usys.ini file.
